profile.name: Enrique Cantillo Villamizar
profile.title: Senior Frontend Engineer — React, Next.js, TypeScript, Design Systems, Testing, Agile
profile.summary: With over a decade of experience building and maintaining web applications, I have worked through different generations of frontend technology — from hand-coded HTML, CSS, and JavaScript to React, Next.js, TypeScript, and AI-assisted development tools. I focus on accessible user experiences, performance, scalable architecture, and interfaces that align with user needs and business goals.

companies.name: Qualmity

Led frontend for a configurable Health & Safety platform with a reusable design system (~40% faster page loads)
Modernized compliance and screening apps with Next.js and Django REST APIs
Built Torque Sphere website and e-commerce experience (reported 10% sales increase)
Code reviews and mentoring junior developers in React, Next.js, and Material UI

companies.name: Sembo (Stena Line Travel Group)

Implemented Jest and Cypress testing; optimized mobile load times from 3.2s to 1.8s
Mentored junior developers and conducted code reviews

topics.answer: I'm Enrique Cantillo Villamizar, a Senior Frontend Engineer based in Berlin with over a decade of experience. I work with React, Next.js, TypeScript, design systems, testing, and agile product teams — currently at Qualmity on Health & Safety, compliance, and screening products. Previously at Sembo (travel), Signum/DISA (compliance & design systems), Eyevinn (video/media), and IBM iX/Aperto (enterprise & accessibility).

Since May 2024 at Qualmity (Berlin, remote), I improve and maintain large B2B applications across Health & Safety, Risk Management, Pre-Employment Screening, and Compliance. Recent work includes leading frontend for a configurable Health & Safety platform with a reusable design system (~40% faster page loads), modernizing screening workflows with Next.js and Django REST APIs, and building the Torque Sphere e-commerce site.

I mentor junior developers through code reviews and hands-on guidance — at Qualmity on React, Next.js, and Material UI; at Sembo on component-based architecture; and at IBM iX/Aperto where I supported a colleague transitioning into frontend development. I focus on clear component boundaries, maintainable patterns, and practical frontend best practices.

I've worked in Scrum, Kanban, and Design Thinking environments at IBM iX/Aperto, Eyevinn, Signum, Sembo, and Qualmity — including sprint planning, daily standups, retrospectives, backlog refinement, and design workshops. I write clear tickets, focused PRs, and documentation that supports async collaboration across distributed teams in Germany, Sweden, and international organizations.

At IBM iX/Aperto (2017–2020) I delivered enterprise web applications for multiple clients using React and the in-house VEAMS framework. I implemented WCAG 2.1 AA compliant components, reusable UI patterns, and participated in Design Thinking workshops. I also mentored a junior developer entering frontend engineering.

I use AI-assisted development tools to explore ideas, improve code structure, debug faster, and document decisions — while keeping architectural judgment and code review standards in human hands. It's part of how I work today alongside React, Next.js, and TypeScript, not a replacement for engineering discipline.
